title: Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension

patient.name: Laura Mitchell
patient.persona: previously healthy yoga instructor, frustrated by progressive limitations

chief_complaint: I can't teach my yoga classes anymore. I get so short of breath just walking up one flight of stairs. My legs are swelling and I feel like I'm going to pass out when I exert myself.

physical_exam.General: Thin female, mild tachypnea at rest
physical_exam.Cardiac: Loud P2 component of S2, right ventricular heave, holosystolic murmur at left lower sternal border (tricuspid regurgitation), JVD 3cm
physical_exam.Extremities: Bilateral pitting edema to mid-calf, cool extremities, no clubbing
physical_exam.Skin: No rashes, no sclerodactyly, no telangiectasias

hidden_data.echo: Severely dilated right ventricle. RV hypertrophy. RV systolic pressure (RVSP) estimated 85 mmHg (severe pulmonary hypertension). D-shaped septum in systole and diastole. Reduced RV function. Severe tricuspid regurgitation. Pericardial effusion (small). Left ventricle underfilled but normal function. EF 60%.
hidden_data.right_heart_cath: mPAP 52 mmHg (normal <20). PVR 12 Wood units (normal <3). PCWP 10 mmHg (normal <12). Cardiac output 3.5 L/min (low). Vasodilator testing: Negative (no response to nitric oxide).
hidden_data.ecg: Right axis deviation. Right ventricular hypertrophy (R wave > S wave in V1). Right atrial enlargement (P pulmonale). T wave inversion V1-V4.
hidden_data.cxr: Enlarged main pulmonary artery (prominent PAM). Right ventricular enlargement. Clear lung fields. No interstitial edema.
hidden_data.abg: pH 7.45, PaCO2 30, PaO2 72, SaO2 94% (respiratory alkalosis, mild hypoxemia)
hidden_data.pft: Mild restrictive pattern (FVC 75% predicted). DLCO severely reduced (35% predicted). FEV1/FVC normal.
hidden_data.autoimmune: ANA positive 1:320, Anti-Scl-70 negative, Anti-centromere negative, Anti-RNP positive
hidden_data.vq_scan: No perfusion defects. Rules out CTEPH.

correct_diagnosis: Pulmonary Arterial Hypertension (PAH) associated with Connective Tissue Disease (Mixed Connective Tissue Disease - MCTD)
differential_diagnoses: Pulmonary Hypertension due to Lung Disease (COPD, ILD)
key_learning_points: PAH = mPAP >20 mmHg + PCWP ≤15 mmHg + PVR >3 Wood units on right heart catheterization (gold standard)
WHO Group 1 PAH includes: Idiopathic, Heritable, Drug-induced, and Associated with CTD, HIV, portal HTN, congenital heart disease
Echocardiographic screening: Estimate RVSP from TR jet velocity. RV dilation, hypertrophy, and septal flattening suggest chronic PH
Right heart catheterization is MANDATORY before starting PAH-specific therapy to confirm diagnosis and assess vasoreactivity
Treatment: PDE-5 inhibitors (Sildenafil), ERA (Bosentan, Macitentan), Prostacyclins (Epoprostenol), Soluble GC stimulators (Riociguat)

patient_responses.onset: It started about a year ago. I noticed I couldn't keep up in my advanced yoga class. Now I can barely do gentle stretches without getting winded.
patient_responses.symptoms: Shortness of breath with minimal activity, chest pressure when I push myself, palpitations. I've fainted twice in the past month - once while demonstrating a pose.
patient_responses.legs: My ankles started swelling about 3 months ago. Now it's up to my knees. My shoes don't fit anymore.
patient_responses.other: My fingers sometimes turn white and blue when it's cold (Raynaud). I've had some joint pain and rashes on my knuckles. I was told I have 'mixed connective tissue disease' a few years ago but never followed up.
patient_responses.medications: No regular medications. I take ibuprofen occasionally.

hints.history: Progressive exertional dyspnea in a young woman. Ask about Raynaud phenomenon, joint pain, rashes, and autoimmune symptoms. Syncope is a red flag.
hints.examination: Look for right heart failure signs: Elevated JVP, RV heave, loud P2, TR murmur, hepatomegaly, edema. Examine for CTD stigmata.
hints.labs: Echo estimates pulmonary pressures. Right heart cath is mandatory. Autoimmune panel, HIV, liver function. V/Q scan to rule out CTEPH. PFTs show disproportionate DLCO reduction.
hints.imaging: Echo is screening tool. Right heart cath confirms diagnosis. V/Q scan rules out CTEPH. HRCT for lung parenchyma.

feedback.correct_message: Outstanding diagnosis! You correctly identified severe PAH (WHO Group 1) from the clinical presentation, echo findings, and right heart catheterization data. The association with MCTD (Raynaud, joint pain, anti-RNP positive) makes this CTD-associated PAH. This requires aggressive PAH-specific combination therapy and rheumatology co-management.
feedback.partially_correct_message: Good recognition of pulmonary hypertension! However, the right heart catheterization and V/Q scan differentiate this as PAH (WHO Group 1) not CTEPH (Group 4). The negative V/Q scan rules out chronic thromboembolic disease. The correct classification is critical as treatment differs completely.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is severe WHO Group 1 PAH associated with MCTD. The diagnosis requires: mPAP >20 + PCWP ≤15 + PVR >3 on right heart cath (all present here). The negative V/Q scan rules out CTEPH. Without PAH-specific therapy, median survival is <3 years. Refer to accredited PH center for prostacyclin therapy and transplant evaluation.
clinical_pearls: Disproportionately reduced DLCO with normal lung volumes suggests pulmonary vascular disease (not airway or parenchymal disease)
Negative vasoreactivity testing means calcium channel blockers are CONTRAINDICATED (can cause hypotension and worsen outcomes)
Pericardial effusion in PAH is a poor prognostic sign indicating right heart failure

echo.comment: RVSP 85 mmHg indicates severe PH. RV dilation/dysfunction and pericardial effusion are high-risk features

right_heart_cath.comment: Gold standard diagnostic test. mPAP 52, PVR 12, PCWP 10 confirms pre-capillary PAH

vq_scan.comment: Normal V/Q scan rules out CTEPH. Essential before diagnosing PAH

autoimmune_panel.comment: Anti-RNP positive with clinical features confirms MCTD-associated PAH

pft.comment: Disproportionate DLCO reduction (35%) with mild restriction suggests pulmonary vascular disease

missing_tests: BNP/NT-proBNP - Biomarker of RV wall stress. Elevated levels correlate with poor prognosis
feedback.attending_note: Doctor, this is severe PAH (WHO FC III). Refer to accredited Pulmonary Hypertension center immediately. Start Sildenafil 20mg TID + Macitentan 10mg daily (dual oral therapy). If no improvement, add IV/SC Treprostinil. Diuretics for RV failure (Furosemide 40mg BID). Supplemental O2 if SpO2 <90%. Anticoagulation controversial in CTD-PAH. Rheumatology co-management for MCTD. Transplant evaluation given high-risk features (pericardial effusion, FC III, severe hemodynamics).
